meta.title: 9 Days Golden Triangle + Shimla from Malaysia | Dhesu Travel & Tours
meta.description: 9-day tour with Delhi, Taj Mahal, Jaipur and Shimla hill station plus toy train, private vehicle and guide. From RM2598 per person.
meta.keywords: 9 Days Golden Triangle + Shimla from Malaysia, India tour package, North India tour package Malaysia, Dhesu travel

hero.title: 9 Days, Triangle & Hills.
hero.titleAccent: Delhi, the Taj, Jaipur and the Shimla Hills.
hero.intro: The icons and the hills in one grand tour. The Golden Triangle of Delhi, Agra and Jaipur, then up to the cool colonial charm of Shimla and the Himachal foothills. Monuments, palaces and mountains over nine days.
hero.heroImageAlt: Golden Triangle and Shimla hills tour

overview.heading: 9 Days Golden Triangle + Shimla: The Full Picture
body: This 9 day tour combines the monumental Golden Triangle with the cool hill station of Shimla. You experience Delhi's history, the Taj Mahal at sunrise and Jaipur's royal forts, then head north into the Himachal foothills for the colonial charm and pine forests of Shimla.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip links the plains and the hills with your own vehicle and driver guide, and the option of the famous Kalka-Shimla toy train. It is ideal for travellers who want the iconic monuments plus a refreshing mountain finish, all at a comfortable pace.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Golden Triangle Icons
items.body: Delhi's monuments, the Taj Mahal at sunrise, Agra Fort and Jaipur's hilltop forts and Pink City palaces, the classic North India trio.

items.title: Shimla's Colonial Hills
items.body: The Mall Road, the Ridge and the pine-clad slopes of the former summer capital of British India, a cool, scenic contrast to the plains.

items.title: The Kalka-Shimla Toy Train
items.body: The UNESCO-listed narrow-gauge railway winds through tunnels and bridges into the hills, a charming and scenic journey.

days.title: Arrive Delhi
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel.

days.title: Delhi, Drive to Agra
days.description: Old and New Delhi, then drive to Agra.

days.title: Taj Mahal, Drive to Jaipur
days.description: Sunrise Taj, Agra Fort, then to Jaipur via Fatehpur Sikri.

days.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
days.description: A full day in the Pink City.

days.title: Jaipur to Delhi to Chandigarh
days.description: Travel north towards the hills, overnight near Chandigarh.

days.title: To Shimla
days.description: Drive or toy train up to Shimla.
suggestedActivities: Toy train or drive to Shimla

days.title: Shimla Sightseeing
days.description: A full day in and around Shimla.

days.title: Shimla Leisure & Descend
days.description: More of Shimla, then descend towards Chandigarh.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Monuments and Mountains, One Journey
whyBook.body: Linking the Golden Triangle with the Shimla hills takes good routing across plains and mountain roads. We sequence it so the journey flows and the toy train slots in. Dhesu has run North India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Golden Triangle & Shimla Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 9 day package pairs the monumental Golden Triangle with the cool hill station of Shimla, giving you India's greatest northern icons plus a refreshing mountain finish.

A private vehicle and driver guide, with the option of the Kalka-Shimla toy train, link the plains and the hills at a comfortable pace.

For a Kashmir finish instead, see our Golden Triangle plus Kashmir packages; for Manali, the 10 day Golden Triangle plus Manali.

sections.heading: Adding the Hills to the Golden Triangle
sections.body:
After the heat and crowds of the plains, the cool pine-scented air of Shimla is a welcome change. The former summer capital of British India offers colonial charm, mountain views and a relaxed pace.

The Kalka-Shimla toy train, a UNESCO World Heritage railway, makes the journey into the hills an experience in itself, a memorable finish to a Golden Triangle tour.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Delhi and Chandigarh Airports (DEL / IXC). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: October to March suits the Golden Triangle, while Shimla is pleasant March to June and snowy December to February. Spring and autumn offer good weather across both the plains and the hills, making them the best windows.

items.question: Is the toy train included?
items.answer: The Kalka-Shimla toy train can be included as part of the hill journey, subject to availability. Seats are in demand, so we book ahead where possible and confirm the segment in your itinerary.

items.question: How cold is Shimla?
items.answer: Shimla is cool year-round given its elevation, with warm jackets needed in winter when it can snow. Summers are mild, a refreshing escape from the plains. Pack layers, especially after the warmer Golden Triangle leg.
